decisions.reasoning: NVDA was oversized at ~36% of portfolio. Jensen Huang delivered COMPUTEX 2026 keynote on June 1 (Vera Rubin platform in mass production, RTX Spark, DGX Station for Windows) and the stock surged ~6% on the day. With the catalyst now largely priced in and gross long exposure at 93.5% blocking any new positions, trimming 1 share is prudent risk management. NVDA remains a core holding at 15 shares (~34% of portfolio), thesis intact on AI datacenter cycle.

sources.headline: Will Nvidia Stock Rise During June 1 to June 4 From CEO Jensen Huang Participation in COMPUTEX 2026?
Nvidia highlights agentic-physical AI, key partnerships at Computex
Jensen Huang GTC Taipei 2026 Keynote: AI Enters Agentic Era, Vera Rubin Platform and Two New Chips Unveiled

decisions.invalidation: Reversal if Vera Rubin production delays emerge, hyperscaler capex cuts, or export control escalation targeting NVIDIA chips

decisions.reasoning: Added 1 share ahead of WWDC June 8 catalyst. Morgan Stanley (OW, $330 PT) calls WWDC a key catalyst capable of reframing Apple as an AI winner; BofA raised PT to $380 citing agentic AI upside of $15-30B in AI revenue by FY2030. The rebuilt Siri with Google Gemini integration is widely expected in iOS 27. Stock trading flat vs our avg entry, indicating WWDC upside is not yet priced. Averaged entry down from $312.60 to $312.03 across 6 shares.

sources.headline: Apple WWDC 2026 Key Catalyst For AAPL Stock, Will Decide AI Positioning: Morgan Stanley

decisions.invalidation: Siri/AI reveal at WWDC June 8 underwhelms (incremental rather than breakthrough); profit-taking pressure if expectations already elevated by event day

decisions.reasoning: Build 2026 conference today delivered strong AI announcements (Office 365 Copilot Agent Mode, GitHub Copilot autonomous coding agent, Azure AI Foundry). Stock up ~5.45% on the event. No FOMO rule: do not add into a +5% gap-up. Position is profitable (+5%) and thesis intact on Azure AI momentum ($37B ARR, +123% YoY).

decisions.invalidation: Azure margin compression accelerates; Build 2026 announcements fail to gain enterprise traction; Fed rate hikes re-emerge on sticky PCE inflation

decisions.reasoning: All SMAs signal long. S&P 500 hit new ATHs in April after Q1 correction, consensus 2026 EPS of $305 at 26.5x P/E implies year-end ~8,100 (+15%). Broad uptrend intact. No trim warranted.

decisions.invalidation: Fed pivots hawkish on sticky PCE (currently 3% vs 2% target); earnings miss vs $305 EPS consensus

decisions.reasoning: Fast SMA ($726.64) well above slow SMA ($697.72), signal long. Tech sector catalysts this week (COMPUTEX, Build 2026, WWDC ahead) supportive of QQQ. Small position kept as broad tech hedge alongside individual names.
decisions.invalidation: Broad tech selloff on macro shock or rate surprise

summary: First session: trimmed 1 NVDA (concentration+catalyst priced), added 1 AAPL (WWDC June 8 catalyst not yet priced); held MSFT/SPY/QQQ; day P/L +$62.73
open_theses: AAPL: WWDC June 8 — rebuilt Siri with Gemini, agentic AI reveal; Morgan Stanley $330 / BofA $380 PT; hold through event
NVDA: Vera Rubin in mass production, COMPUTEX week Jun 1-5 — maintain 15-share core position; monitor ex-div Jun 4
MSFT: Build 2026 AI announcements ($37B ARR, Azure +31%) validate long thesis — hold into next earnings
SPY/QQQ: Broad market uptrend, ATHs, year-end target ~8100 on S&P — keep as portfolio ballast
